title: iberis ciliata subsp. bernardiana
intro: Iberis ciliata subsp. bernardiana, also known as Candil de Azahar, is a type of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, Iberis ciliata subsp. bernardiana has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Iberis ciliata subsp. bernardiana include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
